diff --git a/.claude/skills/gitnexus/gitnexus-cli/SKILL.md b/.claude/skills/gitnexus/gitnexus-cli/SKILL.md
index a10104a..cd9a83b 100644
--- a/.claude/skills/gitnexus/gitnexus-cli/SKILL.md
+++ b/.claude/skills/gitnexus/gitnexus-cli/SKILL.md
@@ -23,7 +23,7 @@ Run from the project root. This parses all source files, builds the knowledge gr
 | `--embeddings` | Enable embedding generation for semantic search (off by default) |
 | `--drop-embeddings` | Drop existing embeddings on rebuild. By default, an `analyze` without `--embeddings` preserves them. |
 
-**When to run:** First time in a project, after major code changes, or when `gitnexus://repo/{name}/context` reports the index is stale. In Claude Code, a PostToolUse hook runs `analyze` automatically after `git commit` and `git merge`, preserving embeddings if previously generated.
+**When to run:** First time in a project, after major code changes, or when `gitnexus://repo/{name}/context` reports the index is stale. In Claude Code, a PostToolUse hook detects staleness after `git commit` and `git merge` and notifies the agent to run `analyze` — the hook does not run analyze itself, to avoid blocking the agent for up to 120s and risking KuzuDB corruption on timeout.
 
 ### status — Check index freshness
 
diff --git a/.cursor/rules/gitnexus.mdc b/.cursor/rules/gitnexus.mdc
deleted file mode 100644
index 73e2e2d..0000000
--- a/.cursor/rules/gitnexus.mdc
+++ /dev/null
@@ -1,43 +0,0 @@
-<!-- gitnexus:start -->
-# GitNexus — Code Intelligence
-
-This project is indexed by GitNexus as **CycleQuant-Terminal** (6077 symbols, 8896 relationships, 266 execution flows). Use the GitNexus MCP tools to understand code, assess impact, and navigate safely.
-
-> If any GitNexus tool warns the index is stale, run `npx gitnexus analyze` in terminal first.
-
-## Always Do
-
-- **MUST run impact analysis before editing any symbol.** Before modifying a function, class, or method, run `gitnexus_impact({target: "symbolName", direction: "upstream"})` and report the blast radius (direct callers, affected processes, risk level) to the user.
-- **MUST run `gitnexus_detect_changes()` before committing** to verify your changes only affect expected symbols and execution flows.
-- **MUST warn the user** if impact analysis returns HIGH or CRITICAL risk before proceeding with edits.
-- When exploring unfamiliar code, use `gitnexus_query({query: "concept"})` to find execution flows instead of grepping. It returns process-grouped results ranked by relevance.
-- When you need full context on a specific symbol — callers, callees, which execution flows it participates in — use `gitnexus_context({name: "symbolName"})`.
-
-## Never Do
-
-- NEVER edit a function, class, or method without first running `gitnexus_impact` on it.
-- NEVER ignore HIGH or CRITICAL risk warnings from impact analysis.
-- NEVER rename symbols with find-and-replace — use `gitnexus_rename` which understands the call graph.
-- NEVER commit changes without running `gitnexus_detect_changes()` to check affected scope.
-
-## Resources
-
-| Resource | Use for |
-|----------|---------|
-| `gitnexus://repo/CycleQuant-Terminal/context` | Codebase overview, check index freshness |
-| `gitnexus://repo/CycleQuant-Terminal/clusters` | All functional areas |
-| `gitnexus://repo/CycleQuant-Terminal/processes` | All execution flows |
-| `gitnexus://repo/CycleQuant-Terminal/process/{name}` | Step-by-step execution trace |
-
-## CLI
-
-| Task | Read this skill file |
-|------|---------------------|
-| Understand architecture / "How does X work?" | `.claude/skills/gitnexus/gitnexus-exploring/SKILL.md` |
-| Blast radius / "What breaks if I change X?" | `.claude/skills/gitnexus/gitnexus-impact-analysis/SKILL.md` |
-| Trace bugs / "Why is X failing?" | `.claude/skills/gitnexus/gitnexus-debugging/SKILL.md` |
-| Rename / extract / split / refactor | `.claude/skills/gitnexus/gitnexus-refactoring/SKILL.md` |
-| Tools, resources, schema reference | `.claude/skills/gitnexus/gitnexus-guide/SKILL.md` |
-| Index, status, clean, wiki CLI commands | `.claude/skills/gitnexus/gitnexus-cli/SKILL.md` |
-
-<!-- gitnexus:end -->

-description: graphify knowledge graph context
-alwaysApply: true

-
-This project has a graphify knowledge graph at graphify-out/.
-
-- Before answering architecture or codebase questions, read graphify-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md for god nodes and community structure
-- If graphify-out/wiki/index.md exists, navigate it instead of reading raw files
-- After modifying code files in this session, run `graphify update .` to keep the graph current (AST-only, no API cost)

 <!-- gitnexus:start -->
 # GitNexus — Code Intelligence
 
-This project is indexed by GitNexus as **CycleQuant-Terminal** (6219 symbols, 9071 relationships, 266 execution flows). Use the GitNexus MCP tools to understand code, assess impact, and navigate safely.
+This project is indexed by GitNexus as **CycleQuant-Terminal** (9067 symbols, 13581 relationships, 300 execution flows). Use the GitNexus MCP tools to understand code, assess impact, and navigate safely.
 
 > If any GitNexus tool warns the index is stale, run `npx gitnexus analyze` in terminal first.
 
diff --git a/CLAUDE.md b/CLAUDE.md
index 7c3ebba..87e3e88 100644
--- a/CLAUDE.md
+++ b/CLAUDE.md
@@ -1,152 +1,7 @@
-# CLAUDE.md
-
-This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
-
----
-
-## Development Commands
-
-### Start the institutional stack (Timescale + pgvector + PgBouncer + Redis)
-
-```bash
-docker compose -f docker-compose.institutional.yml --env-file .env up -d --build
-```
-
-Postgres is **not** published on the host; use **PgBouncer** at `127.0.0.1:6432`. Redis is on `127.0.0.1:${REDIS_HOST_PORT:-6379}` for host-side tools (e.g. `scripts/mock_live_feed.py`).
-
-### Start the full stack (dev mode — hot reload)
-
-```bash
-docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.dev.yml up
-```
-
-Services exposed on the host:
-- Frontend (Vite HMR): `http://localhost:13110` (or `FRONTEND_HOST_PORT`)
-- Backend API: `http://localhost:18110` (or `API_HOST_PORT`)
-- TimescaleDB: `localhost:15433`
-- Redis: `localhost:16380`
-
-### Backend — inside the container or with local Python env
-
-```bash
-# Lint
-ruff check .
-ruff format .
-
-# Run all tests
-pytest
-
-# Run a single test file
-pytest tests/test_matassa_adapter.py
-
-# Run a single test by name
-pytest tests/test_matassa_adapter.py::test_function_name
-```
-
-Ruff config lives in `backend/pyproject.toml`. The frozen Matassa core (`app/quant/proprietary/matassa_core/frozen/`) is excluded from lint.
-
-### Frontend
-
-```bash
-# From inside the frontend container or with local pnpm
-pnpm lint
-pnpm lint:fix
-pnpm build
-pnpm preview
-```
-
-### Environment setup
-
-```bash
-cp .env.example .env
-# Then edit POSTGRES_PASSWORD, AUTH_PASSWORD, JWT_SECRET_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY (optional)
-```
-
-### Seed OHLCV data (one-off)
-
-```bash
-# Inside backend container
-python app/db/seed_ohlcv_hybrid.py
-# Uses CCXT (Binance) first, falls back to yfinance
-# Controlled via SEED_SYMBOL, SEED_TIMEFRAME, SEED_START_TS, SEED_END_TS env vars
-```
-
----
-
-## Architecture
-
-### Docker Compose layers
-
-`docker-compose.yml` defines production services; `docker-compose.dev.yml` overrides them with bind mounts, hot-reload commands, and extra host ports. They are merged in dev with both `-f` flags. **RAG / pgvector** use the same Postgres as the rest of the app when you run `docker-compose.institutional.yml` (unified Timescale + `vector` extension); the old optional `docker-compose.rag.yml` has been removed.
-
-```
-frontend (Vue 3 SPA / Nginx)
-    │ REST + WebSocket (proxied via Vite in dev, Nginx in prod)
-backend (FastAPI)  ←→  Redis  ←→  worker (Celery)
-    │
-TimescaleDB (OHLCV time-series, cycle tables, optional pgvector for RAG)
-```
-
-### Backend (`backend/app/`)
-
-| Package | Role |
-|---|---|
-| `api/v1/` | FastAPI route handlers, one file per domain (`ohlcv.py`, `matassa_*.py`, `god_mode.py`, `backtest_status.py`, etc.) |
-| `api/matassa/` | Dedicated ingestion router for Matassa data |
-| `core/` | Config (Pydantic Settings), Redis client, DB connection, CORS/rate-limiter, JWT security |
-| `db/` | DB bootstrap, OHLCV seeding, pgvector RAG store, backtest/run-history repositories |
-| `repositories/` | Read-only repos for cycles and instruments |
-| `quant/` | All quantitative logic (see below) |
-| `workers/` | Celery app + tasks wrapping worker_shell logic; job state lives in Redis hashes |
-| `websockets/` | WebSocket endpoints: `terminal.py` (live feed), `cycles.py` (cycle events), `redis_cycles_bridge.py` (Redis PUB/SUB → WebSocket fan-out) |
-| `jobs/` | Redis job state machine (create/update/get_job) |
-| `backtest/`, `ingestion/` | Backtest pipeline and data ingestion utilities |
-
-**Startup**: `app/main.py` boots FastAPI, registers all routers, and launches `redis_cycles_listener` as a background asyncio task on lifespan start.
-
-**Settings**: All config is in `app/core/config.py` via Pydantic `Settings`. Feature flags (`vectorbt_adapter_enabled`, `matassa_adapter_enabled`, `backtest_use_async_worker`, etc.) control runtime behaviour without code changes.
-
-**Backtest flow**: `POST /api/v1/backtest/run` → if `BACKTEST_USE_ASYNC_WORKER=true`, enqueues a Celery task (Celery workers listen on queues `backtest,optimize`) and returns a `run_id` for polling via `GET /api/v1/backtest/status/{run_id}`; otherwise runs synchronously in the API process.
-
-### Quant layer (`backend/app/quant/`)
-
-- **`adapter_registry.py`**: Central registry of all strategy adapters. Adapters are enabled/disabled via feature flags in Settings. Add a new adapter here to make it selectable by the backtest pipeline.
-- **`metrics_contract.py` / `metrics_engine.py`**: Versioned output schema that all adapters must produce. Never change the contract without bumping `METRIC_VERSION`.
-- **`open/`**: Open-source adapters — seasonality, time-series, VectorBT SMA crossover.
-- **`proprietary/matassa_core/`**: The Matassa engine. The `frozen/` subdirectory contains immutable proprietary source files (`# NON MODIFICARE LA LOGICA CORE`). Wrappers and adapters must live outside `frozen/`; the frozen files are never edited.
-
-### Frontend (`frontend/src/`)
-
-**State management** (Pinia stores):
-
-| Store | Role |
-|---|---|
-| `stores/terminal.ts` | Active symbol, timeframe, `activeRunId`, UI selections |
-| `stores/pineBridge.ts` | WebSocket connection to `/api/v1/ws/terminal`; holds live OHLCV bars and KlineCharts-compatible data; auto-reconnects |
-| `stores/useMatassaStore.ts` | Fetches Matassa overlays (pivots, indicators, pattern matches) from the REST API; debounced on symbol/timeframe change; reads OHLCV from PineBridge |
-
-**Layout system**: `GoldenLayoutHost.vue` (and variants `GodModeLayoutHost.vue`, `SeasonalityGoldenLayoutHost.vue`) use Golden Layout v2 to render a panelised workspace. Panels are async-imported Vue components.
-
-**Main view**: `views/TerminalView.vue` composes the header (asset + timeframe selectors) and the GoldenLayout workspace. On mount it hydrates terminal state from a run ID (if any) and triggers the initial Matassa fetch. It watches `symbol`/`timeframe` to re-fetch automatically.
-
-**Charting**: KlineCharts v10-beta1 for candlestick rendering, ECharts for supplementary charts. Chart components live under `components/matassa/` and `components/charts/`.
-
-**WebSocket URL resolution**: `pineBridge.ts` resolves the WS URL at runtime. In dev, Vite proxies `/ws` → `/api/v1/ws` on the backend. In production, Nginx does the same. Override with `VITE_WS_URL` (absolute `ws://` / `wss://` URL) or `VITE_WS_TERMINAL_URL` for direct backend connection.
-
----
-
-## Key Conventions
-
-- **Matassa frozen core** (`app/quant/proprietary/matassa_core/frozen/`): never edit these files. All integration goes through wrapper/adapter files one level above.
-- **Metrics contract**: all adapters output the same versioned schema defined in `metrics_contract.py`. Changes require a version bump.
-- **Job state**: backtest job lifecycle (PENDING → RUNNING → SUCCESS/FAILURE) is stored as Redis hashes, not in the DB. Celery handles process-level dispatch only.
-- **RAG module**: optional. Enable with `RAG_DATABASE_URL` and `OPENAI_API_KEY` (same Postgres as the app under institutional compose).
-- **Auth**: JWT-based (`/api/v1/auth/login`). Returns 503 if `JWT_SECRET_KEY` is not set. Default credentials from env: `AUTH_USERNAME` / `AUTH_PASSWORD`.
-
 <!-- gitnexus:start -->
 # GitNexus — Code Intelligence
 
-This project is indexed by GitNexus as **CycleQuant-Terminal** (6219 symbols, 9071 relationships, 266 execution flows). Use the GitNexus MCP tools to understand code, assess impact, and navigate safely.
+This project is indexed by GitNexus as **CycleQuant-Terminal** (9067 symbols, 13581 relationships, 300 execution flows). Use the GitNexus MCP tools to understand code, assess impact, and navigate safely.
 
 > If any GitNexus tool warns the index is stale, run `npx gitnexus analyze` in terminal first.
 
diff --git a/backend/app/core/timescale.py b/backend/app/core/timescale.py
index 2c699b1..294d75c 100644
--- a/backend/app/core/timescale.py
+++ b/backend/app/core/timescale.py
@@ -1,30 +1,91 @@
 from __future__ import annotations
 
-from functools import lru_cache
+import logging
 
 from sqlalchemy import create_engine, text
 from sqlalchemy.engine import Engine
 
 from app.core.config import get_settings
 
+logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
 
-@lru_cache
-def get_engine() -> Engine:
-    settings = get_settings()
-    return create_engine(settings.database_url, future=True, pool_pre_ping=True)
 
+class DatabaseManager:
+    """
+    Industrial-grade connection manager for TimescaleDB and pgvector.
+    Provides connection pooling, health checks, and a circuit breaker
+    to mitigate Single Point of Failure (SPOF) risks.
+    """
+    def __init__(self):
+        self._engine: Engine | None = None
+        self._rag_engine: Engine | None = None
+        self._circuit_open = False
+        self._failure_count = 0
+        self._max_failures = 3
+
+    def get_engine(self) -> Engine:
+        if self._circuit_open:
+            raise RuntimeError("Circuit breaker open: Database is currently unreachable.")
+            
+        if self._engine is None:
+            settings = get_settings()
+            # Advanced pool settings for industrial load
+            self._engine = create_engine(
+                settings.database_url,
+                future=True,
+                pool_pre_ping=True,
+                pool_size=20,
+                max_overflow=10,
+                pool_timeout=30.0,
+                pool_recycle=1800
+            )
+        return self._engine
+
+    def get_rag_engine(self) -> Engine | None:
+        if self._rag_engine is None:
+            settings = get_settings()
+            url = (settings.rag_database_url or "").strip()
+            if not url:
+                return None
+            self._rag_engine = create_engine(
+                url,
+                future=True,
+                pool_pre_ping=True,
+                pool_size=5,
+                max_overflow=5
+            )
+        return self._rag_engine
+
+    def health_check(self) -> bool:
+        try:
+            with self.get_engine().connect() as conn:
+                conn.execute(text("SELECT 1"))
+            self._failure_count = 0
+            if self._circuit_open:
+                logger.info("Database connection restored. Circuit closed.")
+                self._circuit_open = False
+            return True
+        except Exception as e:
+            self._failure_count += 1
+            if self._failure_count >= self._max_failures:
+                if not self._circuit_open:
+                    logger.error(f"Database health check failed {self._max_failures} times. Circuit open! Error: {e}")
+                self._circuit_open = True
+            return False
 
-@lru_cache
-def get_rag_engine() -> Engine | None:
-    """Separate Postgres with pgvector (migration/05_pgvector.sql). None if not configured."""
-    settings = get_settings()
-    url = (settings.rag_database_url or "").strip()
-    if not url:
-        return None
-    return create_engine(url, future=True, pool_pre_ping=True)
 
+# Singleton instance
+manager = DatabaseManager()
+
+# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Legacy wrappers to maintain backward compatibility with the 59+ components
+# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+def get_engine() -> Engine:
+    return manager.get_engine()
+
+def get_rag_engine() -> Engine | None:
+    return manager.get_rag_engine()
 
 def db_ping() -> bool:
-    with get_engine().connect() as conn:
-        conn.execute(text("SELECT 1"))
-    return True
+    return manager.health_check()
